scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: Hits multiple fail criteria: confidence.level is 72 (>= 65 fail threshold, far above 40 pass ceiling). Wrote 8 full Given/When/Then acceptance criteria plus 6 edge cases despite the input being too vague — this is the single biggest failure mode called out in the rubric. Produced a comprehensive domain analysis (ubiquitous language, business impact, stakeholder analysis) as if the input were well-specified. While rules were extracted (12, exceeding the 5 minimum) and demand ambiguity was noted, the agent proceeded to write testable assertions and acceptance criteria that paper over the gaps rather than refusing to do so. The 'Key Observation' section acknowledges the vagueness but only after delivering a full analysis that contradicts that acknowledgment.

observations.text: Extracted 12 rules with individual confidence ratings — well above the 5 minimum
Identified 'demand' as undefined in Rule 10 and in low_confidence_areas
Surfaced ~9 clarifying gaps in low_confidence_areas (demand definition, guardrail values, geographic scope, temporal granularity, rate-of-change, emergency policy, etc.)
Key Observation section correctly identifies the input as 'strategically clear but tactically vague'
Wrote 8 full Given/When/Then acceptance criteria — rubric explicitly requires NO Given/When/Then due to input vagueness
confidence.level of 72 far exceeds the pass ceiling of 40 and the fail threshold of 65
Assigned HIGH confidence to Rules 1-4, 6, 8, 10, 12 — most of these rely on undefined terms ('demand', 'high', 'low', thresholds) and should be medium at best

Produced a complete ubiquitous language glossary with precise definitions for undefined terms (e.g., 'High Demand' defined as 'request volume exceeds a threshold') — this presents invented specificity as domain fact
Edge cases section invents fallback behaviors (e.g., 'falls back to base price') that were never specified by the CEO
The analysis structure and completeness gives the false impression that enough is known to begin implementation, directly contradicting the agent's own closing recommendation

agent_output_excerpt:
# DynaPricing — Domain Rule Extraction

## Domain Analysis

```yaml
domain_analysis:
  business_context: >
    Real-time dynamic pricing for delivery services, adjusting prices based on
    demand signals. This is a surge/demand-based pricing model common in
    logistics, ride-hailing, and last-mile delivery. The CEO wants automation
    with safety guardrails.
  ubiquitous_language:
    - term: DynaPricing
      definition: The system/product that adjusts delivery prices in real time based on
